blurb: Apple designs its A-series and M-series SoCs with integrated security subsystems including dedicated hardware for encryption, key storage, and secure boot. Each SoC contains a hardware-verified Boot ROM, a hardware AES engine accelerating data encryption at line speed, and the Secure Enclave processor running its own trusted OS (sepOS). The chip architecture enforces strong isolation between the application processor and security-critical components via hardware memory controllers and IOMMU, preventing software – including operating system and kernel-level code – from directly accessing protected hardware regions or cryptographic key material.
